what_it_does: Keeps the otherwise-empty extensions directory under version control and states that the folder is for self-authored, build-gated, reversible extensions loaded via import.meta.glob.
observations: File is a .gitkeep placeholder in an extensions directory
Contains a single comment describing the intended contents and loading mechanism
No executable code or symbols are present
standouts: Explicitly labels extensions as 'brain-authored, build-gated, git-reversible'
conventions: Uses .gitkeep to preserve an empty directory in Git
Uses a comment header to document directory purpose
notes: This file is a directory placeholder that also documents the governance and loading model for custom extensions.
questions: What build gate is applied to these extensions?
How are extensions discovered and registered at runtime?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches: it keeps the directory tracked and documents the extension loading approach.
intention_signals: Establish a controlled, reversible extension point where developers can add self-coded modules that are vetted by the build and loaded dynamically.
novelty: Combines a .gitkeep placeholder with a concise manifesto for extension authorship and loading.
contribution: Preserves the extensions directory in version control and records the intended loading and governance policy for custom extensions.
summary_for_embedding: A .gitkeep file in the extensions directory that documents self-coded, build-gated, git-reversible extensions loaded via import.meta.glob.
intention_for_embedding: To maintain an extension directory and declare how custom, reversible extensions are intended to be authored and loaded.
